claim: Independent cross-check confirms the explorer's claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit via SHA-256 file snapshots before and after full-tool Claude agents, converting any detected project mutations into BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that keep PRD readiness blocked.

evidence.summary: fileDigest() uses createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex') to hash file contents
evidence.why: This confirms SHA-256 is the hash algorithm used for file snapshots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() walks all project files, skips allowed_write_roots, and creates SHA-256 digest map
evidence.why: Independent verification that the snapshot mechanism correctly excludes artifact directory and hashes all other project files.

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore snapshot captured before agent loop execution begins
boundaryAfter snapshot captured after all agents complete; diffBoundarySnapshots() compares and assigns 'violated' status if changes detected
evidence.why: Independent verification of the post-execution comparison and violation detection logic.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers array maps each change to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker; finalReadiness merges blockers and sets prd_ready=false if any exist
evidence.why: Confirms the blocker conversion and PRD readiness enforcement mechanism.

evidence.summary: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS array includes Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash - all mutation-capable tools; DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS.permissions.allow grants all these tools
evidence.why: Independent confirmation that Claude agents have full tool access including file mutation tools.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates agent writing unexpected-project-write.txt during execution, asserts blocked status and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Independent test evidence that boundary detection works and blocks readiness even when agent output would otherwise pass.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' initializes git repo, creates untracked-mutation.txt, asserts boundary detects it as 'added' and blocks
evidence.why: Independent verification that git-backed file tracking works and detects untracked file additions.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence has only non-project scopes
evidence.why: Independent confirmation that brownfield project facts require project-scoped evidence.

risks: If allowed_write_roots is misconfigured to include project directories, mutations could be excluded from boundary checks.
If git ls-files fails or walkFiles() has permission issues, files might be excluded from snapshots.
Time-of-check-to-time-of-use gap exists between snapshot creation and PRD readiness determination if other processes modify files.

result.notes: Cross-check independently verified all 15 evidence claims from explorer. SHA-256 snapshots, before/after comparison, full-tool agent permissions, blocker conversion, and test coverage are all confirmed at cited line locations. No conflicts found. The boundary audit implementation matches the explorer's claim exactly.
